author: geunhwan
title: smoke_ConvertCPULayerTest primType is unexpected : unknown_f32 Expected : ref_f32
signature: primType is unexpected unknown Expected ref|Code Issue

failed_jobs.name: CPU functional tests / CPU functional tests
failed_jobs.symptom: smoke_ConvertCPULayerTest_4D_Dynamic ACL variant fails: primType is unexpected : unknown_f32 Expected : ref_f32

key_errors:
src/plugins/intel_cpu/tests/functional/utils/cpu_test_utils.cpp:248: Failure
Value of: primTypeCheck(primType)
  Actual: false
  Expected: true
primType is unexpected : unknown_f32 Expected : ref_f32
root_cause: The CPU functional test smoke_ConvertCPULayerTest_4D_Dynamic with ACL primitive expects the primitive type to be ref_f32, but the actual primitive type reported is unknown_f32. This indicates that a Convert node (f32->i8) dispatched via the ACL backend is being handled by an unexpected implementation. PR #36892 reverts GPU RMS normalization changes, which should be unrelated to CPU Convert layer behavior. This test failure may be a pre-existing flaky test or a regression introduced by a different concurrent merge-queue entry, not by this revert.
reproduction_steps: Run: ov_cpu_func_tests --gtest_filter=*smoke_ConvertCPULayerTest_4D_Dynamic*primitive=acl* on ARM64 cross-compiled build
recommended_actions: Check whether this test was already failing on master before this PR
Run the failing test in isolation to confirm reproducibility
If it is not caused by this revert PR, re-run the failed job
If consistently failing, investigate ACL backend dispatch for Convert f32->i8 with nchw format on ARM64
prevention_strategies: Add this ACL Convert test to the expected_failures list if confirmed flaky. Add a CI check that verifies ARM64 ACL primitive dispatch for Convert layers after any CPU plugin or ACL integration changes.
historical_context: No prior occurrences of this specific primType unknown_f32 pattern found in the investigation database.
